title: Rosemary Focaccia
intro: Paul Hollywood made the nation fall for proper bread. The Thermomix mixes and kneads this dimpled, olive-oil-rich focaccia for you — all you do is dimple and bake.
ingredients: Rosemary and flaky salt, to top
steps: Place water, yeast and sugar in the bowl. Warm 2 min / 37°C / speed 2 until foamy.
Add the flour, salt and olive oil. Mix 6 sec / 0°C / speed 6.
Knead 3 min / dough mode; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
Tip into an oiled tray, cover and prove 1 hour until puffy. Dimple all over with oiled fingers, drizzle with oil, scatter rosemary and flaky salt; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
Bake in a preheated oven at 220°C for 22–25 minutes until golden. (The Thermomix makes the dough; baking is done in your oven.); finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
tips: Don't skimp on the olive oil — it's what makes focaccia.
Press your dimples deep so they hold little pools of oil.

faq.q: Can the Thermomix knead focaccia dough?
faq.a: Yes — dough mode kneads this wet dough in 3 minutes; you only shape and bake.

faq.q: Is this the original Paul Hollywood recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Paul Hollywood and focaccia; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.

faq.q: Can I make this Rosemary Focaccia in a TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The method is written with TM6 and TM7 settings, using metric weights, clear times, temperatures and speeds.

description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Paul Hollywood-style focaccia: metric ingredients, exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
